The UN Refugee Agency (UNHCR) warned that Lebanon’s humanitarian crisis remains “highly fragile” despite the ceasefire announced on 17 April, as Israeli strikes, demolitions, evacuation orders, and restrictions on civilian movement continue across southern Lebanon and parts of the Bekaa Valley.

UNHCR said at least 380 people have been killed since the ceasefire took effect, while hundreds of housing units have been destroyed or damaged.

Responding to Reuters, the UAE official said: "These fabrications are part of a calculated pattern of deflection – shifting blame to others to evade responsibility for their own actions – and are intended to prolong the war and obstruct a genuine peace process."

Ethiopia also issued a statement denying the accusations.
